stocks.name: Wipro Limited
stocks.explanation: Wipro fell to a fresh 52-week low after JPMorgan downgraded the Indian IT services firm to Underweight and cut its price target to $1.70, arguing consensus earnings remain too optimistic as generative AI pressures pricing for traditional IT outsourcing. Weak recent quarterly results and cautious management guidance compounded the selling. The ADR has persistently trailed peers like Infosys and the broader Asia ADR index through June. The move stood out as a stock-specific slide on an otherwise mixed tape.

stocks.name: Reddit Inc.
stocks.explanation: Reddit surged on renewed optimism around its AI data-licensing leverage, with the company reportedly pushing Google and OpenAI for higher, dynamic pricing on 2027 contract renewals versus roughly $50-60 million per deal today. Surging user engagement and expanding advertising partnerships added fuel, and Reddit is set to feature in a July 7 digital-advertising trends call alongside Meta, Google, and AppLovin. Heavy retail chatter on WallStreetBets amplified the move. The whipsaw action reflects the post-IPO momentum playbook for a name debated on its own platform.

stocks.name: Corning Inc.
stocks.explanation: Corning tumbled as investors took profits after an AI-driven parabolic run that pushed its trailing P/E past 105x, far above sector norms and leaving the stock priced for perfection. Much of the prior rally had been mechanical, tied to index-tracking funds buying as Corning moved into growth-focused FTSE Russell indexes. Bearish signals mounted, including roughly $54 million of insider selling over three months and an unusually large trader buying near-term put options. The pullback snapped an overstretched, momentum-led advance.

stocks.name: Teradyne Inc.
stocks.explanation: Teradyne dropped about 7-12% amid a broad semiconductor-equipment selloff, as investors rotated out of chip names following a record first half in which the sector surged more than 80%. Profit-taking hit the group after cautious industry outlooks and Bank of America’s warning of building bubble risk in the AI trade. A New York Times report that OpenAI may delay its IPO to 2027 further pressured near-term AI chip-demand expectations. Test-and-measurement names like Teradyne were caught in the sector-wide derisking.

stocks.name: KLA Corporation
stocks.explanation: KLA fell sharply as the semiconductor equipment complex sold off in unison, giving back gains after an exceptionally strong first half. The decline followed Bank of America’s caution on AI-trade bubble risk and the OpenAI IPO-delay report that dented near-term chip-demand sentiment. As a chip-metrology leader, KLA is highly sensitive to swings in capital-spending expectations across foundries and memory makers. The drop was concentrated profit-taking rather than a company-specific catalyst.

stocks.name: Rapid7 Inc.
stocks.explanation: Rapid7 jumped after Guggenheim’s John DiFucci upgraded Salesforce and ServiceNow to Buy, arguing the AI-disruption fear that gutted software valuations this year had gone too far. The read-through lifted the broader SaaS group, and beaten-down security-software names like Rapid7 rallied on the reassessment. DiFucci acknowledged AI risks are real but said the darkest scenario was already priced in. The move was a sector-wide sentiment rebound rather than a Rapid7-specific announcement.

stocks.name: ZoomInfo Technologies
stocks.explanation: ZoomInfo rallied alongside the broader software-as-a-service group as investors reassessed AI-disruption fears that had compressed enterprise-software multiples all year, spurred by Guggenheim upgrades of Salesforce and ServiceNow. As one of the most heavily shorted and deeply discounted SaaS names, ZoomInfo is prone to outsized moves when sector sentiment turns. Its Q1 print in May had modestly beaten expectations with revenue of about $310 million. No company-specific catalyst was identified for the July 1 pop, which appears sector-driven.

stocks.name: Micron Technology
stocks.explanation: Micron swung lower in a volatile stretch despite reporting blowout fiscal Q3 results, with revenue more than quadrupling to $41.46 billion and guidance of roughly $50 billion for the current quarter on surging AI memory demand. The stock initially spiked about 15% on the print before reversing as the broader semiconductor selloff and rising AI-infrastructure-cost concerns took hold. A report that OpenAI may push its IPO to 2027 added to the derisking across chip names. The disconnect highlighted how positioning, not fundamentals, is driving near-term chip volatility.

stocks.name: Applied Materials
stocks.explanation: Applied Materials fell about 10% in the semiconductor-equipment selloff as investors booked profits following a record first half for the sector. Cautious industry outlooks and Bank of America’s warning about AI-trade bubble risk drove the rotation out of chip names. The OpenAI IPO-delay report further weighed on near-term capital-spending expectations across the group. As a leading wafer-fab-equipment supplier, Applied Materials is closely tied to foundry and memory capex sentiment.

stocks.name: Lam Research
stocks.explanation: Lam Research dropped alongside its equipment peers as the chip complex derisked after an outsized first-half rally. The selling was driven by profit-taking, Bank of America’s bubble-risk caution on the AI trade, and the OpenAI IPO-delay headlines that pressured near-term demand assumptions. Lam’s heavy exposure to memory-maker capital spending makes it especially sensitive to shifts in AI-infrastructure sentiment. The move reflected sector-wide positioning rather than a Lam-specific development.

stocks.name: AppLovin Corporation
stocks.explanation: AppLovin extended its rally after Raymond James launched coverage with a Strong Buy and a $640 target, citing AI-driven ad growth, rising e-commerce advertising, and strong free-cash-flow generation. Citi reiterated Buy and lifted its target to $710, pointing to the June 30 general availability of AppLovin’s Axon e-commerce platform as a fresh revenue leg beyond gaming. The stock has climbed sharply from the mid-$440s in recent weeks. It is also slated for a July 7 digital-advertising trends call.

stocks.name: Coinbase Global
stocks.explanation: Coinbase rallied as Wall Street desks reiterated bullish views following its latest System Update event, with one firm backing a $270 target as the platform expands into a financial super app. New launches include tokenized stocks, options on crypto and equities, U.S. equities and ETF access, and an AI-powered in-app advisor. Strengthening crypto-market activity and Cathie Wood’s ARK adding roughly 111,000 shares reinforced positive sentiment. Investors are also anticipating a renewal of the USDC partnership with Circle in August.

stocks.name: Intel Corporation
stocks.explanation: Intel fell about 7-9% as chip stocks retreated broadly after Bank of America flagged growing bubble risk in the AI trade, with AMD and Taiwan Semiconductor also lower. The pullback came after a torrid run that left Intel up roughly 278% year-to-date, making it vulnerable to profit-taking. Underlying concerns persist around its 18A-P process, where manufacturing yields remain below profitable thresholds until late 2026 or 2027. The drop was sentiment-driven amid the sector-wide derisking.

stocks.name: Meta Platforms Inc.
stocks.explanation: Meta jumped as much as 9.7% — its sharpest single-day move of the year — after Bloomberg reported the company is building a cloud business to sell access to its AI computing power and models, setting up a collision with AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud. The plan follows aggressive infrastructure moves, including locking in roughly 1.6 gigawatts of Crusoe compute and a $21 billion CoreWeave deal. Q1 revenue of $56.3 billion, up 33%, underpins the spending. Meta is also investing $900 million in India’s Cred and pushing new AI hardware.

stocks.name: Celsius Holdings
stocks.explanation: Celsius Holdings rose nearly 9%, recovering some ground after a steep year that left it well below its 52-week high despite booming fundamentals. First-quarter revenue jumped 138% year-over-year to $782.6 million and net income more than doubled to $110.1 million. Several major firms including UBS, BofA, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan maintain positive ratings even as they trim price targets. The bounce reflected bargain-hunting in a high-growth energy-drink name that still trades roughly 55% below its peak.
